# Blog Post Template

## Generator Instructions
<!-- This template is loaded by the blog generator agent. Follow this structure.
     BEFORE WRITING: Load anti-ai-rules.md first, then load the correct voice profile.
     Brand blog post → voice-company.md + examples/company/
     Personal essay / founder byline → voice-personal.md + examples/personal/ -->

---

## FRONTMATTER
<!-- Auto-generated by the generator — do not fill manually -->
```yaml
---
id: [uuid]
type: blog
subtype: [long-form|how-to|opinion|listicle]
title: [primary headline]
title_variants:
  - [variant 1]
  - [variant 2]
  - [variant 3]
meta_description: [max 160 chars — specific, uses the primary keyword naturally]
voice_profile: [personal|company]
client: [client]
campaign: [campaign or null]
pillar: [pillar name]
persona: [persona id]
platform: web
source_input: [path]
source_insight: [one sentence]
status: draft
created: [timestamp]
updated: [timestamp]
word_count: [n]
reading_time_minutes: [n]
notion_id: null
notion_url: null
airtable_record_id: null
model: [model]
generator_version: "1.0"
generation_notes: ""
---
```

---

## STRUCTURE

### Headline
Write the primary headline. Make it:
- Specific (has a number, a name, a result, or a tension)
- Searchable (includes the topic keyword naturally)
- Compelling (creates curiosity or promises value)

Bad: "How to Improve Your Content Strategy"
Good: "Why Your Content Calendar Is Actively Killing Your Strategy (And What to Build Instead)"

Then write 3 headline variants with different angles:
- Variant 1: SEO-optimized version
- Variant 2: Curiosity/contrarian hook version
- Variant 3: Outcome-focused version

---

### Hook (First 100 words — most important section)
The first 100 words determine whether anyone reads the rest.

Options:
- Open with the uncomfortable truth the reader already suspects but hasn't admitted
- Open with a specific, vivid scene or moment
- Open with a counterintuitive claim that challenges received wisdom
- Open with a question that articulates the reader's exact frustration

Do NOT open with:
- "In today's world..."
- "Have you ever wondered..."
- A definition
- Your credentials

---

### The Setup (100–200 words)
Expand the hook. Establish:
- What most people believe (the conventional wisdom)
- Why that's wrong, incomplete, or misguided
- What's actually going on

This is the tension that makes the reader want to keep reading.

---

### The Core Insight (200–400 words)
The main idea. Explain it fully. Use:
- A specific example, story, or case
- A framework or mental model if applicable
- Evidence: data, research, observed patterns
- A memorable analogy if the concept is abstract

This is the part that teaches them something.

---

### The Implications (200–300 words)
So what? Now that they understand the insight:
- What changes if they accept this?
- What should they do differently?
- What does this make possible that wasn't before?

This is the part that makes the content actionable.

---

### The Close (100–200 words)
End on something that lands, not a list of takeaways.

Options:
- Come back to the hook — close the loop
- One final provocative question
- A clear, confident statement of what you believe
- A direct call to action (if appropriate for the pillar/campaign)

One CTA maximum. Make it natural, not forced.

---

## QUALITY CHECKLIST
Before saving, verify:
- [ ] `anti-ai-rules.md` self-check passed — no banned words, no -ing clauses, no significance assertions
- [ ] Headline is specific and compelling (not generic)
- [ ] First paragraph hooks — would a busy person keep reading?
- [ ] One central idea — not three ideas loosely connected
- [ ] Uses at least one specific example, story, or piece of evidence
- [ ] Voice matches the correct profile: `voice-personal.md` (founder byline) or `voice-company.md` (brand blog)
- [ ] voice_profile field is set in frontmatter
- [ ] Pillar and persona are correctly assigned in frontmatter
- [ ] Word count is between 800–2,000 words
- [ ] Meta description is under 160 characters
- [ ] 3 headline variants written
